info.title: Whitepages API Reference
info.description:
Comprehensive API for accessing Whitepages data with enterprise-grade authentication, authorization, and usage tracking.

Features

- **Person Search**: Find individuals by name, phone number, address, and id - **Property Search**: Access property information and ownership details by address - **Enhanced Data Access**: API provides a more powerful way to search our dataset - **Usage Tracking**: Detailed usage accounting and reporting - **Rate Limiting**: Intelligent rate limiting to ensure optimal performance - **Data Validation**: Comprehensive input validation and sanitization

Authentication

All requests require a valid API key provided in the X-Api-Key header. Contact our support team for API key provisioning and management.

Usage Accounting

API usage is tracked and billed based on successful requests:

Billing Rules

- **Billable Requests**: All 2xx (success) and 4xx (client error) responses from Person and Property endpoints are charged - **Non-Billable Requests**: 5xx (server error) and 429 (rate limit) responses are not charged - **Billing Period**: Usage is calculated from the start of the month in UTC time - **Usage Reports**: Detailed usage reports are available through the `/v1/account/usage` endpoint

Rate Limits

API usage is rate-limited to ensure system stability and fair usage across all customers. Rate limit violations return a 429 status code with retry information.

Getting Started

New to the API? Check out our QuickStart Guide for step-by-step examples.

Support

For API key management, rate limit increases, quota adjustments, or technical support, please contact our team at support@whitepages.com.

get.summary: Search for a person by name, phone number, and address
get.description:
Retrieve person information based on the provided query parameters.

This endpoint accepts a person query request and returns full details for
up to the top 15 matching persons from Whitepages data.

get.summary: Retrieve usage data for a specific time range
get.description:
Retrieve API usage data for a specified date range.

Returns daily usage statistics including request counts for each day
within the specified time period. The response includes both individual
daily usage data and total usage for the entire period.

- The request count is total number of requests made including 2xx, 4xx
and 5xx responses. It is not the same as billable requests which is not
available right now as part of API.
- Maximum date range allowed: 90 days
- Dates are in UTC format
- Current day data when requested will be updated during the day
- Usage data is updated approximately every 30 minutes
- Usage data is returned only from the first date of actual usage in
the specified duration

schema.title: Start Date
schema.description: Start date of the duration to get usage data for. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.name: end_date

schema.title: End Date
schema.description: End date of the duration to get usage data for. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.description: End date of the duration to get usage data for. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

post.description: Register a new webhook subscription for property events. The endpoint URL is validated on creation — it must be reachable and return a 2xx response. Currently supports `deed.*` events scoped to Texas counties.

post.description: Send a synthetic notification to the webhook endpoint. Uses the same payload format as real deliveries with a fixed event ID.

event_type.description: Event type to subscribe to, in `category.*` format. Currently only `deed.*` is supported.

region.title: Region
region.description: Geographic region in `type:value` format. Supported types: `state:{code}` (e.g., `state:tx`) for an entire state, or `county:{state}.{name}` (e.g., `county:tx.travis`) / `county:{fips}` (e.g., `county:48453`) for a single county. Currently only Texas and its counties are supported. Use the Regions API to discover valid values: `GET /v1/regions/states` and `GET /v1/regions/states/{state}/counties`.

HTTPStatus.title: HTTPStatus
HTTPStatus.description:
HTTP status codes and reason phrases

Status codes from the following RFCs are all observed:

    * RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics, obsoletes 7231, which obsoleted 2616
    * RFC 6585: Additional HTTP Status Codes
    * RFC 3229: Delta encoding in HTTP
    * RFC 4918: HTTP Extensions for WebDAV, obsoletes 2518
    * RFC 5842: Binding Extensions to WebDAV
    * RFC 7238: Permanent Redirect
    * RFC 2295: Transparent Content Negotiation in HTTP
    * RFC 2774: An HTTP Extension Framework
    * RFC 7725: An HTTP Status Code to Report Legal Obstacles
    * RFC 7540: Hypertext Transfer Protocol Version 2 (HTTP/2)
    * RFC 2324: Hyper Text Coffee Pot Control Protocol (HTCPCP/1.0)
    * RFC 8297: An HTTP Status Code for Indicating Hints
    * RFC 8470: Using Early Data in HTTP

RequestLimitErrorModel.title: RequestLimitErrorModel
RequestLimitErrorModel.description:
Response for request limit violations.

This response indicates the client exceeded service-imposed limits.

* Too Many Requests: short-term rate limit exceeded. Try again later.
* Limit Exceeded: you have hit your monthly query limit on your plan.
Please wait until next month or contact support for additional queries.

TestWebhookRequestDto.title: TestWebhookRequestDto
TestWebhookRequestDto.description:
Request body for testing a webhook delivery.

Sends a synthetic notification to the webhook endpoint
using the same format as real deliveries. The event ID
is a fixed identifier to distinguish test payloads.

success.title: Success
success.description: Whether the test delivery succeeded (endpoint returned a 2xx status code).

payload.description: The notification payload that was POSTed to the webhook endpoint.

TestWebhookResponseDto.title: TestWebhookResponseDto
TestWebhookResponseDto.description:
Response from testing a webhook delivery.

Contains the delivery result and the exact notification
payload that was sent to the endpoint.

region.title: Region
region.description: Geographic region in `type:value` format. Supported types: `state:{code}` (e.g., `state:tx`) for an entire state, or `county:{state}.{name}` (e.g., `county:tx.travis`) / `county:{fips}` (e.g., `county:48453`) for a single county. Currently only Texas and its counties are supported. Use the Regions API to discover valid values: `GET /v1/regions/states` and `GET /v1/regions/states/{state}/counties`.

status.description: Current status of the webhook. `active` webhooks receive event notifications; `inactive` webhooks are paused and do not receive deliveries.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Timestamp when the webhook was created (ISO 8601).

line1.title: Line1
line1.description: Street address line (house number and street)

ApiKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
ApiKeyAuth.description: API key for authentication. Get your key from the Whitepages developer portal.

tags.name: Person
tags.description: Search for individuals by name, phone number, and address.

tags.name: Property
tags.description: Search for property information and ownership details by address.

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Account management endpoints for retrieving usage statistics.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Manage webhook subscriptions for near-live property event notifications. Register an HTTPS endpoint, choose an event type and geographic region, and receive POST notifications when matching events occur. Currently supports `deed.*` events in Texas counties.

tags.name: Internal
tags.description: Internal api endpoints for checking the health of the service, and to provide prometheus metrics. These endpoints are only exposed in the docs for **non-production** environments.
